title: New active compounds against pathogenic microorganisms
abstract: A series of linear peptides is described which are useful for the treatment of pathologies caused by pathogenic microorganisms. Said peptides have a significant antibacterial activity, associated with great stability and resistance to the action of bacterial peptidases, and low or non-existent hemo- or cyto-toxicity. In particular, the disposition of the hydrophobic amino acids alternated with charged amino acids gives the peptides high activity.
claims:
Claims 1. A composition comprising one or more peptides, chosen from a group that comprises the peptides having the sequences KIFKKIRKAGI IAKII (SEQ ID NO.:l), KIFK ILKILKKILKWI (SEQ ID NO.:2), KIFKKIWKILKKILKWI (SEQ ID NO.:3), KIIKKIWKIIKKIL WI (SEQ ID NO.: 4), GTWWKKWGWIKTRLGGL (SEQ ID NO.: 5). 2. The composition in claim 1, for use in medicine. 3. The composition in claims 1 or 2, for the prevention and/or treatment of infectious pathologies in man and animals. 4. The composition according to any of the claims from 1 to 3, wherein at least one peptide of said one or more peptides is a linear peptide. 5. The composition according to any of the claims from 1 to 4, wherein at least one peptide of said one or more peptides is composed of one or more D-amino acids. 6. The composition according to any of the claims from 1 to 5, wherein at least one peptide of said one or more peptides is composed of one or more non-natural amino acids. 7. The composition according to any of the claims from 1 to 6, for the prevention and/or treatment of pathologies caused by microorganisms including bacteria, funguses and/or yeasts, viruses and protozoa. 8. The composition according to any of the claims from 1 to 6, for the prevention and/or treatment of pathologies caused by bacteria. 9. The composition according to claim 8, wherein the antimicrobial activity is directed against at least one of the microorganisms selected from the group of pathogens consisting of Staphylococcus spp., for example Staphylococcus aureus (e.g. Staphylococcus aureus ATCC 25923), Enterococcus spp., for example Enterococcus faecal is; Pseudomonas spp., for example Pseudomonas aeruginosa ATCC 27853, or Pseudomonas marginalis; Mycobacterium spp., for example Mycobacterium tuberculosis; Enter obacter spp.; Campylobacter spp.; Salmonella spp.; Streptococcus spp., for example Streptococcus group 27853 A or B, Streptoccocus pneumoniae, Helicobacter spp., for example Helicobacter pylori; Neisseria spp., for example Neisseria gonorrea, Neisseria meningitidis; Borrelia burgdorferi, Shigella spp., for example Shigella flexneri; Escherichia 

 coli (ATCC 25922); Haemophilus spp., for example Haemophilus influenzae; Francisella tularensis, Bacillus spp., for example Bacillus anthracis; Clostridia spp., Clostridium botulinum, Yersinia spp., for example Yersinia pestis; Treponema spp.; Burkholderia spp., for example Burkholderia cepacia, B. mallei and B pseudomallei; Stenotrophomonas spp., for example Stenotrophomonas maltophilia; Ervinia spp.; Listeria spp.. 10. The composition according to any of the claims from 1 to 6, for the prevention and/or treatment of pathologies caused by funguses and/or yeasts. 1 1. The composition according to claim 10, wherein the anti-fungal activity is directed against at least one of the microorganisms selected from a group of pathogens consisting of Candida spp. (for example C. albicans), Epidermophyton spp., Exophiala spp., Microsporum spp., Trichophyton spp. (Tor example T. rubrum and T. inter digitale), Tinea spp., Aspergillus spp., Blastomyces spp., Blastoschizomyces spp., Coccidioides spp., Cryptococcus spp. (Tor example Cryptococcus neoformans), Histoplasma spp., Paracoccidiomyces spp., Sporotrix spp., Absidia spp., Cladophialophora spp., Fonsecaea spp., Phialophora spp., Lacazia spp., Arthrographis spp., Acremonium spp., Actinomadura spp., Apophysomyces spp., Emmonsia spp., Basidiobolus spp., Beauveria spp., Chrysosporium spp., Conidiobolus spp., Cunninghamella spp., Fusarium spp., Geotrichum spp., Graphium spp., Leptosphaeria spp., Malassezia spp., (Tor example Malassezia furfur), Mucor spp., Neotestudina spp., Nocardia spp., Nocardiopsis spp., Paecilomyces spp., Phoma spp., Piedraia spp., Pneumocystis spp., Pseudallescheria spp., Pyrenochaeta spp., Rhizomucor spp., Rhizopus spp., Rhodotorula spp., Saccharomyces spp., Scedosporium spp., Scopulariopsis spp., Sporobolomyces spp., Syncephalastrum spp., Trichoderma spp., Trichosporon spp., Ulocladium spp., Ustilago spp., Verticillium spp., Wangiella spp.. 12. The composition according to any of the claims from 1 to 1 1, in which at least one peptide of said one or more peptides shows a low or negligible hemolytic activity against erythrocytes derived from man or an animal. 13. The composition according to any of the claims from 1 to 12, in which at least one peptide of said one or more peptides shows a low or negligible cytotoxic activity against epithelium, endothelium or mesenchymal cells of man or an animal. 14. Pharmaceutical composition for use in man or animals, comprising one or more peptides chosen from a group that comprises the peptides having the sequences KIFKKIRKAGIKIAKII (SEQ ID NO.:l), KIFKKILKILKKILKWI (SEQ ID NO.:2), KIFKKIWKILKKILKWI (SEQ ID NO.:3), KIIKKIWKIIKKILKWI (SEQ ID NO.: 4), GTWWKKWGWIKTRLGGL (SEQ ID NO.: 5). 15. Pharmaceutical composition according to claim 14, suitable for topical, oral, subcutaneous or parenteral administration, or by inhalation. 16. Method to synthesize a peptide formed by one or more peptides chosen from a group that comprises the peptides having the following sequences: KIFKKIRKAGIKIAKII (SEQ ID NO.:l), KIFKKILKILKKILKWI (SEQ ID NO.:2), KIFKKIWKILKKILKWI (SEQ ID NO.:3), KIIKKIWKIIKKILKWI (SEQ ID NO.: 4), GTWWKKWGWIKTRLGGL (SEQ ID NO.: 5), wherein said method comprises binding all the amino acids that make up the peptides in a linear sequence.
